as_boundary: Continue rejecting recipient non-init command-message inputs for standard-signal and write-buffer command tokens; standard-signal self-target command execution remains source-blocked, while direct self-mailbox and completed self-target write-buffer execution are implemented by ADR-0161. The accepted recipient behavior is the ADR-0054 rejection boundary, with multi-command conflicts assigned to ADR-0059 reject-and-clear.

implemented_claims.summary: AS now has a named transition claim and proof-certificate entry for the recipient non-init command-message rejection boundary, including explicit standard-signal and write-buffer positive rejection examples.

implemented_traces.summary: AS now has a schematic-linked trace that replays fixed-cell upstream standard-signal command-message rejection.
AS now has a schematic-linked trace that replays fixed-cell direct multi-command recipient rejection.

implemented_svgs.summary: AS now has a rendered SVG view checked exactly against the recipient non-init command-message rejection trace.
AS now has a rendered SVG view checked exactly against the multi-command recipient rejection trace.

implemented_evidence_bundles.summary: AS now has an integrated evidence bundle validating one recipient non-init rejection boundary across claim, proof, trace, render, and source-status layers while explicitly covering both delivered write-buffer rejection examples at the claim/proof layer.

implemented_source_statuses.summary: AS now has a source-status decision and ADR-0161 implementation for direct self-mailbox and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer execution, while recipient write-buffer command-message inputs remain rejected here.
AS now has a source-status decision keeping standard-signal command-token execution blocked across recipient, self-mailbox, and self-target command-buffer surfaces.
AS now has a source-status decision selecting reject-and-clear for multiple simultaneous recipient command-message inputs.

formal_command_table.summary: The formal command table includes standard-signal as a command-buffer command.

legacy_special_message_exclusion.summary: The legacy special-message sets exclude standard-signal and treat standard input as a separate classification.

standard_signal_status.as_boundary: Continue rejecting standard-signal command-message input until AS decides whether standard-signal command tokens replay ordinary binary-input routing, remain unsupported, or have a different recipient/self-target surface.

legacy_divergences.summary: RAA dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through write-buf, which appends only when the buffer-full check permits it; input processing separately clears channels.
SEMSIM defines write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as appends, but stem-process-special-message wraps the selected operation in zero-hig and zero-buf, creating an unclear buffer-clearing boundary.
FSMSIM dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through primitive-eval and appends to the buffer while clearing the self mailbox and input channels.

write_buffer_status.as_boundary: Continue rejecting recipient write-buffer command-message input under the ADR-0054 rejection claim. Direct self-mailbox and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer execution are outside this recipient non-init surface and are implemented by ADR-0161.

multi_command_input_status.summary: multiple simultaneous command-message inputs are assigned the existing reject-and-clear boundary; AS does not prioritize or sequence them.
multi_command_input_status.as_boundary: ADR-0049 consumes exactly one single command-message input token. ADR-0059 selects rejection for two or more simultaneous command-message tokens.

allowed_next_slices: Revisit standard-signal command execution only if new source evidence resolves command-token, binary-input, recipient-surface, and self-target-surface boundaries.
Revisit recipient write-buffer command-message execution only if new source evidence justifies moving it out of the ADR-0054 non-init rejection boundary.
